entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A type of millet, Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R. Br.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of pearl millet to withstand drought conditions.

entities.name: QTL MAPPING
entities.description: A method used to identify quantitative trait loci.

entities.name: QTLS
entities.description: Quantitative trait loci, regions of DNA associated with specific traits.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced by the pearl millet crop.

entities.name: TERMINAL DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Drought conditions occurring during the reproductive stage of pearl millet.

entities.name: LINKAGE GROUP 2 (LG 2)
entities.description: A specific region on the pearl millet genome where a major QTL for grain yield and drought tolerance is located.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding technique using molecular markers to improve traits.

entities.name: GENERAL COMBINING ABILITY (GCA)
entities.description: A statistical measure of the average performance of lines in crosses.

entities.name: INTROGESSION LINES
entities.description: Lines containing introgressed genes from another source.

entities.name: GENETIC MAPS
entities.description: Maps showing the location of genes and markers on chromosomes.

entities.name: NILS
entities.description: Near-isogenic lines, genetically similar lines differing only in the region containing the QTL.

entities.name: GENE SEQUENCES
entities.description: The order of nucleotides in genes.

entities.name: COMPARATIVE GENOMICS INFORMATION
entities.description: Data comparing the genomes of different organisms.

relationships.description: QTL mapping is used to understand drought tolerance.
QTLs are associated with grain yield.
Terminal drought stress affects grain yield.
Terminal drought stress is a condition related to drought tolerance.
LG 2 is associated with grain yield.
LG 2 is associated with drought tolerance.
Marker-assisted backcrossing is used to accumulate desirable QTL alleles.
Introgression lines are used to validate the effect of QTLs.
Gene-based markers are developed within QTL regions.
Genetic maps are developed for mapping drought tolerance traits.
Genetic maps are used to identify and characterize QTLs.
NILs are used to study QTLs.
Gene sequences are used to develop gene-based markers.
Comparative genomics information aids in the development of gene-based markers.
